description: Every legal section cited in any rule. This is the dependency graph's anchor layer. When a Finance Act amends a section, look it up here to find all affected objects.

sections.short_title: Mandatory filing of return of income
sections.description: Governs who must file a return of income and by when. ITR form eligibility conditions are embedded in the instructions issued under this section.
sections.notes: ITR form eligibility conditions change annually via instructions/notifications, not always via Finance Act.

sections.short_title: Special provision for computing profits of business on presumptive basis
sections.description: Allows eligible businesses to declare income at 6% (digital receipts) or 8% (cash) of turnover without maintaining books of accounts.
sections.notes: Finance Act 2023 doubled the digital receipts threshold from ₹1 Cr to ₹3 Cr. This is a frequently amended section — high change risk.

sections.short_title: Special provision for computing profits of profession on presumptive basis
sections.description: Allows specified professionals to declare income at 50% of gross receipts without maintaining books.
sections.notes: Finance Act 2023 increased limit from ₹50L to ₹75L.

sections.short_title: Tax on income of individuals and Hindu undivided family — New Regime
sections.description: Provides for an alternative (new) tax regime with lower slab rates but fewer deductions. Made the default regime from AY2024-25 by Finance Act 2023.
sections.notes: Slabs and standard deduction were updated by Finance Act 2024. Standard deduction raised from ₹50,000 to ₹75,000 under new regime. Rebate u/s 87A extended to ₹12L from AY2025-26.

sections.description: Provides a rebate that effectively makes tax zero for taxpayers below a certain income threshold.
sections.notes: New Regime rebate limit raised to ₹12L (from ₹7L) by Finance Act 2025. This section is frequently amended and has cascading effects on refund computations.
